title: Remote Access Anomalies — Geo-Velocity and Off-Hours VPN

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AC-17 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AC-17 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.24: Remote Access Anomalies — Geo-Velocity and Off-Hours VPN.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.24' running on ms:o365:management SignIns, cisco:asa VPN logs, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620)
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload=AzureActiveDirectory Operation=UserLoggedIn earliest=-24h
| iplocation extended_field=ClientIP
| stats earliest(_time) as t1, latest(_time) as t2, values(Country) as countries by UserId, ClientIP
| eval delta_h=round((t2-t1)/3600,2)
| where mvcount(countries)>1 AND delta_h<2
description: Detects impossible travel and off-hours remote access for AC-17 remote access monitoring.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Validate `UserLoggedIn` operation naming for tenant; (2) pair with MFA status field if present; (3) reduce noise with corporate travel calendar lookup optional; (4) document tuning in SSP. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.
visualization: Map (country hops), Table (impossible travel), Time chart (after-hours VPN). Key fields: `ClientIP`, `UserId`, `countries`, `delta_h`, `t1`, `t2`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:management` SignIns, `cisco:asa` VPN logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Validate `UserLoggedIn` operation naming for tenant; (2) pair with MFA status field if present; (3) reduce noise with corporate travel calendar lookup optional; (4) document tuning in SSP.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload=AzureActiveDirectory Operation=UserLoggedIn earliest=-24h
| iplocation extended_field=ClientIP
| stats earliest(_time) as t1, latest(_time) as t2, values(Country) as countries by UserId, ClientIP
| eval delta_h=round((t2-t1)/3600,2)
| where mvcount(countries)>1 AND delta_h<2
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remote Access Anomalies — Geo-Velocity and Off-Hours VPN (AC-17)** — Detects impossible travel and off-hours remote access for AC-17 remote access monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` SignIns, `cisco:asa` VPN logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:management, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.

• `stats` aggregates events by **UserId, ClientIP**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvcount(countries)>1 AND delta_h<2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Remote Access Anomalies — Geo-Velocity and Off-Hours VPN (AC-17)** — Detects impossible travel and off-hours remote access for AC-17 remote access monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` SignIns, `cisco:asa` VPN logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: ClientIP, UserId, countries, delta_h, t1, t2.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Map (country hops), Table (impossible travel), Time chart (after-hours VPN)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for detects impossible travel and off-hours remote access for remote access monitoring. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
